title: CS2 Launch Options Optimizer — Best Settings for Performance | CSDB.gg
description: Optimize your CS2 launch options for maximum FPS, reduced input lag, and stable performance. Choose from presets or build a custom launch string for your hardware.
heading: CS2 Launch Options Optimizer
intro: Configure your Steam launch options for optimal CS2 performance. Select individual flags or start from a hardware-matched preset.

faqs.question: How do I set launch options in CS2?
faqs.answer: Right-click CS2 in your Steam Library → Properties → General tab → Launch Options. Paste your launch options string in the text field. Changes take effect next time you start the game.

faqs.question: What are the best CS2 launch options?
faqs.answer: For most players: -novid -high -tickrate 128. Add -threads [your CPU threads] on older hardware. Avoid deprecated options like -nojoy or -lv which no longer do anything in CS2.

faqs.question: Does -high actually help in CS2?
faqs.answer: The -high flag sets CS2 to high process priority, which can improve performance on systems running background applications. On dedicated gaming PCs with nothing else running, the difference is minimal.

faqs.question: What launch options reduce input lag?
faqs.answer: The most impactful options for input lag are -high (priority), -d3d9ex (DirectX optimization), and disabling VSync in-game. The -tickrate 128 flag ensures offline practice matches run at full tick rate.

faqs.question: Are there launch options that hurt performance?
faqs.answer: Yes. Options like -fullscreen (forces exclusive fullscreen which can cause issues), too many -threads, or outdated CS:GO options that don't exist in CS2 can cause instability or have no effect.
